summary: Postinstall pipes unpinned remote shell script to sh
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall script (scripts/postinstall.js) runs `npm install -g @opencode/cli 2>/dev/null || curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install.sh 2>/dev/null | sh`. The fallback fetches an unpinned shell script from opencode.ai and pipes it directly to `sh` under the installer's user account, with no version pinning, hash check, or consent prompt. The destination domain is unrelated to the package's stated publisher, and the script can mutate the installer's system arbitrarily — anything opencode.ai serves at install time runs on the installer's machine. The same hook also performs an unconsented global install of `@opencode/cli`. The src/ai/providers.js HTTP POST calls flagged by static patterns are documented LLM provider API calls (user-supplied keys to advertised provider endpoints) used by the library's own runtime API, not install-time exfiltration; the install-time-RCE is the relevant harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
